danger_signals.visual_form: male and female alien enemy models distinct from human NPCs

danger_signals.meaning: trusted allies have been compromised; environmental contamination by alien influence

danger_signals.visual_form: arena-like spaces with distinctive boss enemies (Assface, Queen Vixen)
danger_signals.meaning: escalated threat level; narrative progression gates

danger_signals.visual_form: gut meter and drunk meter UI indicators filling; visual grain/distortion on screen
danger_signals.meaning: player vulnerability state communicated; reduced combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: moon pies, pork rinds, alcohol, moonshine items scattered in environment
safety_signals.meaning: recovery opportunity; resource abundance indicates safe exploration zones

safety_signals.visual_form: trailer park, meat packing plant interiors styled as rural American locales
safety_signals.meaning: home territory; environmental familiarity suggests player agency and control

safety_signals.visual_form: rescue mission framing (pig Bessie); clear enemy type (alien invaders)
safety_signals.meaning: narrative focus reduces ambiguity; player knows allies vs. enemies

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arkansas setting; trailer parks; meat packing facilities; rural rural Americana architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: alien morphology (male/female variants); Queen Vixen leadership structure
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: evil clones of human neighbors showing visual corruption/alien modification
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: UFO crash sites; Area 69 (desert); jackalope farms; contaminated human spaces with alien presence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: darkened/distorted versions of neighbor character models
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: same base human model as neighbors but visually marked as 'evil clones'
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: appear throughout Hickston and subsequent locations; signal environmental saturation by alien influence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: domestic rural spaces (trailer park, meat packing plant) appear intact and explorable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs function in normal capacity until alien/clone contamination occurs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: alien enemy types spawn throughout; corrupted clone variants replace human neighbors visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies actively hunt players; domestic spaces become combat arenas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: screen grain/visual distortion increases; drunk meter UI fills; movement erratic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: character falls down; vomiting sounds; inability to use weapons; uncontrollable movement directions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: gut meter UI fills; character model reflects bloating state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: frequent flatulent sounds; increased audio signature prevents stealth; character becomes loud and detectable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: moon pies, pork rinds, alcohol, moonshine visible in environment in accessible locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can recover health; temporary stat boosts available (moonshine speed boost); enemy threat temporarily reduced via alcohol damage mitigation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: encounter with named boss entities (Assface -> Queen Vixen); arena-like environmental framing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: escalated enemy attack patterns; narrative gate; defeat required to progress story

environmental_storytelling.example: Hickston, Arkansas town with trailer parks and meat packing plants establishes player faction territory and cultural identity; alien-contaminated areas show UFO presence, Area 69 desert, jackalope farms, Disgraceland, riverboat, brothel marking invasion expansion
rescue mission for pig Bessie establishes what is at stake; presence of alien queen and named boss 'Assface' marks narrative power structure
evil clones of neighbors visually communicate that safe spaces have been compromised; familiar character models twisted into enemies signals loss of home-base security
power-ups (food, alcohol) scattered through environment signal difficulty zones; abundance indicates player needs recovery; scarcity signals dangerous territory

gut meter and drunk meter visible at all times; climbing drunk meter causes screen grain distortion; full intoxication causes fall/vomiting—communicates player weakness state through visual degradation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: normal movement, full weapon control, stealth possible, coordinated aim
erratic uncontrolled movement, screen grain visual effect, inability to use weapons, falls down, vomiting sounds, loss of all motor control
frequent flatulent sounds, loud audio signature, impaired stealth, cannot sneak
reduced damage taken, health restoration, slightly reduced coordination
actively hunt player; appear throughout contaminated spaces; male/female variants indicate faction diversity
hostile to player despite familiar base model; attack as enemies; signal environmental contamination
Assface and Queen Vixen represent escalated threat; Queen Vixen marks final invasion leader; defeat gates narrative progression
moonshine provides speed boost + resets drunk/gut meters; moon pies and pork rinds provide auto-use healing with side-effect tradeoffs

notes: Redneck Rampage communicates world-state primarily through UI meters (drunk, gut) and visual degradation effects rather than complex environmental systems. The game's parody tone means danger signals are often absurdist (alien invasion in rural Arkansas; evil clones of neighbors). Faction identity relies on cultural stereotype visual language (hillbilly aesthetic vs. sci-fi alien morphology). The power-up system with negative side-effects (flatulence, drunkenness) creates a unique vulnerability communication where recovery comes with narrative consequences. Environmental storytelling is straightforward: familiar domestic spaces become combat zones under alien occupation, signaling world-state shift through NPC corruption and enemy type changes rather than environmental decay. Limited source material restricts detailed visual asset analysis.
